query_normalized: analyzing the trade‑off between prompt size and code generation quality computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) because it explicitly investigates the impact of scaling input length (prompt size) on code generation performance, which is a core component of the user's trade-off analysis, even though the primary focus is on model architecture and state-of-the-art results. Additionally, it serves as a foundational empirical baseline (c) for Java code generation quality against which prompt-size trade-offs can be evaluated.
